2012.tagline: Tamed Tempests Bring Peace to Palau
2012.narrative: In 2012, Palau was spared the fury of nature as a storm brought no recorded fatalities or material damage. The absence of destruction allowed the country to breathe a sigh of relief. This rare occurrence highlights the importance of preparedness and resilience in the face of natural disasters.

narrative: 2013 was a year of serenity for Palau, with no recorded fatalities or damage from natural disasters.
The country's tropical climate and geography allowed it to avoid the devastating impacts of many other global disasters.
As a result, the people of Palau were able to live in relative peace and security, free from the fear of natural catastrophes.

2015.narrative: In 2015, Palau faced a prolonged drought that lasted for several months. Despite the lack of rainfall, the island nation's crops and ecosystems were able to adapt and survive, thanks to the resilience of its people. The disaster had no reported fatalities or damage, a testament to the strength and preparedness of the Palauan community.

2018.narrative: In 2018, Palau faced a silent threat in the form of a dengue fever epidemic. With swift action and effective response measures, the country was able to contain the outbreak, reporting zero fatalities and zero economic damage. This quiet resilience in the face of uncertainty serves as a testament to Palau's ability to adapt and thrive in the face of adversity.

2021.tagline: Serenity in the face of calm
narrative: In 2021, Palau experienced a period of relative calm, with no recorded fatalities and zero estimated damage from natural disasters.
The year's only significant event was a storm, which posed no threat to human life or infrastructure.
The tranquil conditions allowed Palau to focus on its natural beauty and unique ecosystem, without the added stress of devastating natural disasters.
